title: K000160369: Unable to Discover F5 devices in ServiceNow CMDB

metadata.concepts: F5OS ; ServiceNow ; F5 rSeries ; MIBs ; classifiers ; missing ; custom ; OIDs ; root cause ; CI creation ; lack of out-of-box ; responses ; documentation ; misconfiguration ; configuring
metadata.f5_title: Unable to Discover F5 devices in ServiceNow CMDB
metadata.excerpt: ServiceNow Discovery was unable to identify and catalog F5 rSeries (F5OS) devices in the CMDB. Attempts to discover the devices failed to retrieve required attributes such as the serial number, res...

content.sfdetails__c:
Description

ServiceNow Discovery was unable to identify and catalog F5 rSeries (F5OS) devices in the CMDB. Attempts to discover the devices failed to retrieve required attributes such as the serial number, resulting in classification errors and incomplete CI creation. The issue persisted despite loading F5OS MIB files into ServiceNow and configuring SNMP access, with error messages indicating missing matching attributes for device identification.

Environment

  • F5 rSeries platform running F5OS version 1.8.x.

  • ServiceNow Discovery integration targeting F5OS devices via SNMP.

  • F5OS MIB files loaded into ServiceNow.

  • SNMP manager IPs are added to F5OS allow list.

  • SNMP configured on F5OS (port 161, correct community string/user).

  • Network connectivity established between ServiceNow and F5OS management interface.

Cause

The root cause is a lack of out-of-box support in ServiceNow Discovery for F5OS-based rSeries devices. ServiceNow’s OOB discovery patterns are designed for BIG-IP devices and do not query the required F5OS-specific MIBs/OIDs, resulting in missing device attributes and failed classification. No misconfiguration or defect was identified on the F5OS side.

Answer/Recommended Actions

  • Confirm with ServiceNow support or documentation whether custom discovery patterns can be developed to support F5OS rSeries devices by querying the F5-OS-SYSTEM-MIB::F5OSSystem OID for serial number and other attributes.

  • Monitor ServiceNow product updates for future support of F5OS-based rSeries platforms.

  • If immediate discovery is required, consider developing custom SNMP classifiers or scripts within ServiceNow to retrieve the necessary attributes from F5OS devices.

  • Continue to use OOB patterns for legacy BIG-IP devices, as these are supported.

Additional Information

While ServiceNow is unable to discover the F5OS device, we can still see all the SNMP get requests and corresponding responses in /var/F5/system/log/snmp.log confirming no issue from F5OS device.
